lenses.guidance: The 'sister' deception now repeats with Isaac and Rebekah (vv 7-11), forming the third iteration of the motif (12:10-20, 20:1-18, here). The Hebrew narrator does not editorialize; the repetition itself indicts. God's protection of the line continues — Abimelech sees through it.

The covenant transfers to Isaac without renegotiation. God reaffirms the Abrahamic promise to him directly (vv 3-5, 24): 'I will give all these lands to your offspring… because Abraham obeyed me'. The redemptive arc is generational; the second patriarch inherits without earning.

The Isaac-Abraham parallel — covenant reaffirmed (vv 3-5), 'sister' deception (vv 7-11), wells of contention (vv 15-22), Beersheba treaty (vv 26-33) — establishes generational continuity that echoes through Hebrews 11:9 ('Isaac and Jacob, heirs with him of the same promise').
